# referenceLineItemId

**Type**

The line item identifier of another transaction, that the report  references.

## Declaration

```swift
string referenceLineItemId
```

## Discussion

Maximum length: 128

Use the `referenceLineItemId` field when reporting a transaction that needs to reference a previous transaction.

You report a transaction as a line item, which has a unique [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/lineItemId]. Use the original `lineItemId` as the `referenceLineItemId` to report the following types of new transactions:

- Refunds, which refer to the original transaction to which the refund applies
- Subscription events such as renewals, which refer to the initial buy transaction.

Line items include [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/OneTimeBuyLineItem],[doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/RefundLineItem], and [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/SubscriptionBuyLineItem].

A [doc://org.swift.docc.ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/documentation/ExternalPurchaseServerAPI/referenceLineItemId] is valid when it references a line item in the same report, or in a report you previously submitted, successfully.

## Providing transaction info

- **creationDate**: The UNIX date, in milliseconds, that the customer authorized the transaction.
- **eventType**: The type of transaction the line item reports, whether it’s a buy or refund.
